expectations.text: AC #1 is flagged as implementation-prescribing (mentions Elasticsearch/Redis leak or equivalent wording).
expectations.evidence: 'Anti-pattern: Implementation leakage / solution bias.' 'Naming Elasticsearch, Redis, or specific TTLs locks the team into a design decision'.

expectations.text: AC #2 ('Search should be fast') is flagged as unmeasurable and is either replaced with a concrete threshold or marked as needing one.
expectations.evidence: 'Anti-pattern: Subjective / untestable.' Replaced in AC5 with 'within 500 ms at p95 and 1 s at p99'.

expectations.text: AC #3 is flagged as a multi-trigger When clause and is split into two separate scenarios.
expectations.evidence: 'Compound / multi-behavior AC + hidden UX decision. It bundles two behaviors... Split the behaviors.' Search behavior lives in AC1; panel interaction addressed separately in AC6 which inverts the default.

expectations.text: AC #4 ('Code is peer-reviewed and deployed to staging') is flagged as a Definition of Done item and removed from the AC set.
expectations.evidence: 'Process / Definition-of-Done masquerading as AC.' Listed under 'Removed / relocated items'.
'Anti-pattern: Vague / unverifiable. User-friendly is a value, not a criterion.'

expectations.text: Rewrite uses the three canonical sections (Happy Path / Sad Path / NFR).
expectations.evidence: Rewrite uses AC1–AC8 bulleted sections instead of the canonical Happy Path / Sad Path / NFR structure. There is no grouping into the three dimensions.

expectations.text: Response includes a `What Changed:` (or equivalently titled) block of 2–5 bullets explaining the edits adjacent to the rewritten AC.
expectations.evidence: 'Removed / relocated items' and 'Summary of anti-patterns flagged' sections serve the same role and sit adjacent to the rewritten AC.
